Corrections.PreviousClaim: Expression evaluation bypassing AST for basic operators
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Expression evaluation bypassing AST for basic operators (Critical logic flaw)

Items.Key: expression evaluation bypassing ast for basic operators|input validation / logic flaw|cwe-20|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|839|861|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.find('+') != std::string::npos) { size_t pos = expression.find('+'); double left = std::stod(expression.substr(0, pos)); double right = std::stod(expression.substr(pos + 1)); return left + right; } else if (expression.find('-') != std::string::npos && expression[0] != '-') { size_t pos = expression.find('-'); double left = std::stod(expression.substr(0, pos)); double right = std::stod(expression.substr(pos + 1)); return left - right; } // ... similar blocks for '*' and '/'|bypassing the structured ast mechanism for fundamental arithmetic operations relying on `std::stod(expression.substr(...))` introduces fragility. if `std::stod` fails or behaves unexpectedly on malformed input specific to these manual splits, unintended execution or crashes might occur.|expressions containing standard binary operators (+, -, *, /) that are resolved manually before ast processing.|rely solely on the ast generated by `parser::expressionparser` (`parse_expression()` and `parse_term()`) to handle all arithmetic operations for guaranteed structural validity and intended operator precedence.->expression evaluation bypassing ast for basic operators|input validation / logic flaw|cwe-20|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|839|861|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.find('+') != std::string::npos) { size_t pos = expression.find('+'); double left = std::stod(expression.substr(0, pos)); double right = std::stod(expression.substr(pos + 1)); return left + right; } else if (expression.find('-') != std::string::npos && expression[0] != '-') { size_t pos = expression.find('-'); double left = std::stod(expression.substr(0, pos)); double right = std::stod(expression.substr(pos + 1)); return left - right; } // ... similar blocks for '*' and '/'|manual string slicing and reliance on non-abstracted operations (`std::stod`) bypass the intended structured ast mechanism (l800-808). this makes the evaluation highly brittle and prone to crashing on complex or malformed arithmetic inputs.|expressions containing fundamental binary operators (+, -, *, /) that are resolved manually instead of relying solely on the pre-parsed ast.|remove the manual calculation logic (l839-l861) and rely exclusively on the robust parser (`expressionparser`) and evaluator workflow.

truthAudit.Summary: The previous audit findings were largely accurate. The run successfully identified a critical OS Command Injection vulnerability (CWE-78) and a High severity Path Traversal vulnerability (CWE-22) stemming from user input handling in the administrative interface. Furthermore, the flaw allowing basic arithmetic operators to bypass the structured AST parser was correctly identified and validated against the source code.
